stem: A network performance study measures signal latency for 15 samples to determine if the process variability is below 50ms for 99% of packets. The sample standard deviation is 5.23 ms (sample variance = 27.35). Estimate the true population standard deviation of signal latency and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample SD = 5.23 ms. 95% CI for σ (chi-squared): [3.83, 8.25]. Point estimate: 5.23 ms.

common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (5.23) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
